prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `7c9b52a0`.
codex_response:
The rule is: find the same-sized rectangular windows cut into the large background, treat `0` inside each window as transparent, and overlay each window’s one nonzero fragment into a single zero canvas of the common window size. I used the summary hint as a starting hypothesis: `arc2_opus46_summary.json` was consistent with the official examples, and the `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` entry was also consistent, so no `notes.md` correction was needed.

The task package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7c9b52a0/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7c9b52a0/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7c9b52a0/generator.py). The verifier extracts non-background connected windows and paints each window’s nonzero color into a shared zero canvas; the generator builds that composite first, then emits 2-4 separate zero-filled windows in a 16x16 background using the observed palette/layout family. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation passed: `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7c9b52a0/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7c9b52a0/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7c9b52a0/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7c9b52a0/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7c9b52a0/previews/preview_03.png).
